methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Einride
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Harbinger
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Waabi, a Series C autonomous vehicle software company founded by Raquel Urtasun, is evaluated against a transportation sector comp universe of seven private peers including Einride, Harbinger, Rivian, The Boring Company, and REGENT Craft. No public comparables exist in the relevant segment.

The Stage-Progression method is the primary applicable valuation framework. Using total capital raised of $1.0B as a proxy baseline and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement, this method yields $1.7B. The Peer-Median approach is not applicable: while five same-sector peers exist, the narrow filters for stage-specificity and comparable tier produced insufficient datapoints (fewer than three) to establish a reliable median, and a broad all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.4B to $2.0B, with a median of $1.7B supported by the single applicable method. Key uncertainties center on the limited methodological cross-check available—reliance on a single approach leaves the valuation vulnerable to model specification error. The absence of public market comparables and the sparse same-stage peer set further constrain confidence. Resolution of these uncertainties would require either identification of additional comparable financings or progression toward a later funding event that anchors valuation more directly to market signals.
